Setup and data loading

In [1]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns

from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score, classification_report, confusion_matrix

# Load data
df = pd.read_csv("/Users/sana/Desktop/DTSC 2301/diabetes.csv")
df.head()

Basic info + missing-value handling (zeros → median)

In [2]:
df.info()
df.describe()

# Columns where 0 means "missing"
zero_as_missing = ["Glucose", "BloodPressure", "SkinThickness", "Insulin", "BMI"]

for col in zero_as_missing:
    median_val = df.loc[df[col] != 0, col].median()
    df.loc[df[col] == 0, col] = median_val

df[zero_as_missing].describe()

Exploratory data analysis (EDA)

In [3]:
# Histograms
df.hist(figsize=(12, 8))
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Boxplots by Outcome
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 8))
for i, col in enumerate(df.columns[:-1], 1):
    plt.subplot(3, 3, i)
    sns.boxplot(data=df, x="Outcome", y=col)
    plt.title(col)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Correlation heatmap
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6))
corr = df.corr()
sns.heatmap(corr, annot=True, cmap="coolwarm", fmt=".2f")
plt.title("Correlation Heatmap")
plt.show()

# Sort correlations with Outcome
corr["Outcome"].sort_values(ascending=False)

Train–test split + scaling helper

In [4]:
RANDOM_STATE = 42

target = "Outcome"
all_features = ["Pregnancies", "Glucose", "BloodPressure", "SkinThickness",
                "Insulin", "BMI", "DiabetesPedigreeFunction", "Age"]

X_full = df[all_features]
y = df[target]

X_train_full, X_test_full, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
    X_full, y, test_size=0.2, random_state=RANDOM_STATE, stratify=y
)

def run_experiment(feature_list, name="Experiment"):
    print(f"\n===== {name} =====")
    X_train = X_train_full[feature_list]
    X_test = X_test_full[feature_list]

    # Scale for LR (and optionally others)
    scaler = StandardScaler()
    X_train_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(X_train)
    X_test_scaled = scaler.transform(X_test)

    models = {
        "Logistic Regression": LogisticRegression(max_iter=1000, random_state=RANDOM_STATE),
        "Decision Tree": DecisionTreeClassifier(random_state=RANDOM_STATE),
        "Random Forest": RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=200, random_state=RANDOM_STATE)
    }

    results = {}
    feature_importances = {}

    for model_name, model in models.items():
        if model_name == "Logistic Regression":
            model.fit(X_train_scaled, y_train)
            y_pred = model.predict(X_test_scaled)
        else:
            model.fit(X_train, y_train)
            y_pred = model.predict(X_test)

        acc = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)
        print(f"\n--- {model_name} ---")
        print(f"Accuracy: {acc:.3f}")
        print("Classification Report:")
        print(classification_report(y_test, y_pred, digits=3))

        cm = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred)
        sns.heatmap(cm, annot=True, fmt="d", cmap="Blues")
        plt.title(f"{name} - {model_name} Confusion Matrix")
        plt.xlabel("Predicted")
        plt.ylabel("True")
        plt.show()

        results[model_name] = acc

        # Feature importance for tree-based models
        if hasattr(model, "feature_importances_"):
            fi = pd.Series(model.feature_importances_, index=feature_list).sort_values(ascending=False)
            feature_importances[model_name] = fi

    return results, feature_importances

Experiments

Experiment 1: All features

In [5]:
exp1_features = all_features
exp1_results, exp1_importances = run_experiment(exp1_features, name="Experiment 1: All Features")

===== Experiment 1: All Features =====

--- Logistic Regression ---
Accuracy: 0.708
Classification Report:
              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0      0.752     0.820     0.785       100
           1      0.600     0.500     0.545        54

    accuracy                          0.708       154
   macro avg      0.676     0.660     0.665       154
weighted avg      0.699     0.708     0.701       154


--- Decision Tree ---

    accuracy                          0.682       154

weighted avg      0.673     0.682     0.676       154

           1      0.652     0.556     0.600        54

    accuracy                          0.740       154
   macro avg      0.715     0.698     0.704       154
weighted avg      0.734     0.740     0.735       154

Experiment 2: Remove Glucose

In [6]:
exp2_features = [f for f in all_features if f != "Glucose"]
exp2_results, exp2_importances = run_experiment(exp2_features, name="Experiment 2: No Glucose")

exp3_results, exp3_importances = run_experiment(exp3_features, 
                                                name="Experiment 3: No Glucose, BMI, Age")

Experiment 4: Only “weak” features

exp4_features = ["Pregnancies", "BloodPressure", "SkinThickness", "Insulin", "DiabetesPedigreeFunction"]
exp4_results, exp4_importances = run_experiment(exp4_features, 
                                                name="Experiment 4: Minimal Weak Features")

Comparing performance across experiments

In [9]:
summary = pd.DataFrame({
    "Experiment 1 (All)": exp1_results,
    "Experiment 2 (No Glucose)": exp2_results,
    "Experiment 3 (No Glu/BMI/Age)": exp3_results,
    "Experiment 4 (Weak Only)": exp4_results
})
summary
Out[9]:
Experiment 1 (All) Experiment 2 (No Glucose) Experiment 3 (No Glu/BMI/Age) Experiment 4 (Weak Only)
Logistic Regression 0.707792 0.681818 0.688312 0.688312
Decision Tree 0.681818 0.610390 0.623377 0.623377
Random Forest 0.740260 0.688312 0.668831 0.668831
In [10]:
summary.T.plot(kind="bar", figsize=(10, 5))
plt.ylabel("Accuracy")
plt.title("Model Performance Across Experiments")
plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Feature importance analysis (Random Forest focus)

In [11]:
def plot_rf_importances(importances_dict, title):
    rf_importances = importances_dict.get("Random Forest")
    if rf_importances is None:
        return
    plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
    rf_importances.sort_values().plot(kind="barh")
    plt.title(title)
    plt.xlabel("Importance")
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

plot_rf_importances(exp1_importances, "RF Feature Importance - Exp 1 (All Features)")
plot_rf_importances(exp2_importances, "RF Feature Importance - Exp 2 (No Glucose)")
plot_rf_importances(exp3_importances, "RF Feature Importance - Exp 3 (No Glu/BMI/Age)")
plot_rf_importances(exp4_importances, "RF Feature Importance - Exp 4 (Weak Only)")

When Glucose is present, it likely dominates.

When Glucose is removed, BMI, Age, and maybe Pregnancies/DiabetesPedigreeFunction rise.

When Glucose, BMI, and Age are all removed, the model leans on subtler patterns.
